dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 responding for chest pain.
Hi, I'm Maria, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
I've been having chest pain, trouble breathing, and a fever for about the last two days. It's getting worse.
Can you tell me your name and the date?
My name is Laura, and today is December 30th.
How are you feeling right now, Laura?
I feel like I can't catch my breath. My chest feels tight. It's hard to swallow, and I have body aches.
Do you mind if we check your airway and breathing?
She's breathing a bit fast and she's coughing, so let's take a quick look at her respirations.
Respiration rate: 28. She's tachypneic.
What does your breathing feel like to you? Are you having a hard time getting air?
Yes, I'm short on air and it's hard to breathe deeply. I'm coughing a lot.
Pulse: 58, well-perfused and regular.
Blood Pressure: 109/71. It's stable for now.
Oxygen saturation: 99%. We should continue monitoring and consider delivering supplemental oxygen.
Do you have any other symptoms besides chest pain and shortness of breath?
I have a cough, chills, and body aches. I've also lost my appetite. I feel like I have a fever. I've noticed lesions on my arms and torso, which are not painful or itchy.
I see you have some bumps on your arms and torso. These appeared about two days ago, and they are non-painful, right?
Yes, they're not painful or itchy.
Do you have any pain or tenderness around your neck or behind your ears?
Yeah, my lymph nodes in my neck and around my ears are swollen and tender.
On auscultation, I hear ronchi and wheezes in all fields.
Do you have any medical issues or allergies we should know about?
I have severe asthma, high blood pressure, and had a heart attack years ago.
When did the chest pain feel like it began for you?
It started getting worse over the last two days. It's been constant now for two days.
Does anything make the chest pain better or worse?
It's worse when I cough and when I try to take a deep breath.
How would you describe the pain? Sharp, dull, burning, etc.?
It feels like a heavy pressure.
Does the chest pain spread to your arms, neck, or back?
No, it stays in my chest and behind my sternum area.
On a scale from 0 to 10, with 10 being the worst pain of your life, how bad is your chest pain now?
I'd say it's about a 7.
We can try a combined nebulizer treatment with albuterol and ipratropium to see if your breathing improves.
Glucose: 0. We'll continue with the protocol as blood sugar is not a current issue in management.
Have you used any nebulizer treatments at home recently?
Yes, I tried a Pulmicort nebulizer twice, but it didn't help much.
Looking at your neck: your lymph nodes in the cervical chain appear edematous.
I don't see any changes in the skin color of the neck. It looks unremarkable otherwise.
Respiration rate improved to 18.
Pulse is now 69 and still regular.
Blood Pressure is now 116/75 with slight improvement.
Oxygen saturation now at 100%. Good improvement.
Based on the vitals and EKG results, I am following protocol 3-11 for respiratory distress.
Let's start with a 4-lead EKG monitoring for continuous observation prior to the 12-lead.
We're going to take a second set of vitals and perform a 12-lead EKG.
Let's recheck her EKG to confirm everything looks stable.
Vitals are stable. EKG now reading better, no acute changes detected.
With your consent, we'll be transporting you to [COMPANY_NAME] for further evaluation.
That's fine with me.
You were sitting on the bed. We're going to move you now for transport.
We'll carefully secure you now to help with the transport and minimize discomfort.

She has a known history of asthma, history of myocardial infarction, and high blood pressure. Initially presented with asthma exacerbation showing improvement after nebulizer treatment. She also has edematous lymph nodes in the cervical and post-auricular areas, non-painful lesions on the arms and torso, and symptoms including fever, chills, body aches, cough, and trouble swallowing.
I'm going to check for any other rashes or lesions beyond the areas already noted.
I don't have any other lesions or rashes today.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; EMT
dialogue.utterance: Have you been experiencing any difficulty swallowing or noticing any throat pain?

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; Patient
dialogue.utterance: Yes, I have trouble swallowing and it hurts when I eat.
How long has it been since you last ate or drank anything?
It's been about two to three days. I can't keep food down.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; EMT
dialogue.utterance: You did not mention any nasal drainage, right?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: When did your symptoms first start?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: I've been sick with a cold-like illness since before Christmas, but it has gotten much worse in the last two days.
Your lungs still have ronchi and wheezes. No significant murmurs or chest deformity.
Can you move all your limbs for me? Any pain or numbness?
I'm fine with my arms and legs. I don't feel any numbness.
EKG shows sinus rhythm with a possible old inferior infarct.
No acute ST changes at this time.
We'll continue support on the backboard during transport.
You mentioned no pain or itching with the new lesions. I don't see any pallor to the skin either.
No signs of neck vein distention or nuchal rigidity.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; EMT
dialogue.utterance: No evidence of active pharyngeal obstruction or swollen tonsils.
The pulse was strong and assessed equal bilaterally.
No signs of abnormal breathing sounds over the abdominal region.
Are you experiencing any changes in the frequency or urgency of urination?
No, just loss of appetite.
You mentioned fever and chills. Are they the same or intensifying now?
It still feels like a high fever and the chills are not going away.
We'll stick with the current protocol and monitor for any additional signs before transport.
Are you up for loading now?
Sure. Just make it gentle, please.
We'll stabilize and support the backboards to minimize further distress.
All checks appear complete. We are good to load and begin transport.
Refer to the full medical history and recent improvement after nebulization during handoff to the hospital.
The lesions are non-painful and do not appear to be spreading at the moment.
We note the presence of tender cervical and post-auricular lymph nodes.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; EMT
dialogue.utterance: No other lesions or sores in the oral cavity confirmed.
Breathing seems to be less labored after treatment, so no need for seal bags or oxygen supplementation at this time.
